headline: Disabled Students Face 62% Higher University Dropout Rate
context: Students with disabilities enrolled in public universities drop out at 18% compared to 11% for non-disabled peers.

cards.big: Disabled students drop out of university at 18% versus 11% for non-disabled peers
cards.sub: Physical barriers inadequate support services and social isolation drive attrition

cards.big: Only 4 of 20 public universities have dedicated disability support offices as of 2025
cards.sub: Most institutions handle disability accommodations through general student affairs

cards.big: Persons with Disabilities Act 2008 requires reasonable accommodation but provides no funding
cards.sub: Unfunded mandate means universities bear costs with no additional allocation

cards.big: UK universities receive RM8,400 per disabled student through Disabled Students Allowance
cards.sub: Dedicated per-student funding ensures support services are adequately resourced

cards.big: Admitting disabled students without supporting them is not inclusion—it is performance
Should every public university receive per-student disability support funding from MOE
